title: Daily Temperatures
prompt:
Given an array of integers temperatures represents the daily temperatures, return an array answer such that answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after the ith day to get a warmer temperature. If there is no future day for which this is possible, keep answer[i] == 0.

For example, given temperatures = [73,74,75,71,69,72,76,73], the output is [1,1,4,2,1,1,0,0] because:
- Day 0 (73°): next warmer is day 1 (74°), wait 1 day
- Day 2 (75°): next warmer is day 6 (76°), wait 4 days
- Day 6 (76°): no warmer day exists, answer is 0

notesForAI.coreIdea: Use a monotonic decreasing stack of indices. For each temperature, pop all stack entries with lower temperatures, computing their wait times as the difference in indices. Push the current index.
commonBugs: Storing values on the stack instead of indices
Using a strictly increasing stack instead of decreasing
O(n^2) brute force that checks every future day
